basics.name: Tomoko Sato
basics.label: Senior Product Manager
basics.summary: B2B product leader with 9 years shipping developer tools and collaboration software. Known for crisp PRDs, ruthless prioritization, and building trust across engineering and go-to-market.

work.name: Nimbus Works
work.description: Async collaboration platform for distributed engineering teams
work.summary: Own roadmap for permissions, integrations, and enterprise admin console.
highlights: Launched SCIM provisioning increasing enterprise win rate 22%.
Reduced time-to-first-value 40% via guided onboarding redesign.
Grew MAU from 80k to 310k over three years.

work.name: Rakuten Symphony
work.summary: Managed public API lifecycle for partner developers and internal microservices.
highlights: Shipped developer portal with OpenAPI docs and sandbox environments.
Defined deprecation policy adopted across 200+ internal APIs.

work.name: Accenture
work.summary: Supported digital transformation projects for retail and financial clients.
highlights: Facilitated requirements workshops for ERP migration program.
Built ROI models for mobile banking feature prioritization.

volunteer.summary: Organize monthly product management meetups with 200+ attendees.
highlights: Secured sponsorship from five local tech companies.
Launched mentorship matching program for junior PMs.

awards.title: Product Excellence Award
awards.summary: Recognized for leading enterprise admin console launch that unlocked $4M ARR in first quarter.

publications.name: Prioritization Frameworks That Survive Executive Reviews
publications.summary: Combining RICE, opportunity scoring, and strategic bets for platform PMs.

projects.name: PM Interview Prep JP
projects.description: Bilingual resource site with case studies and mock interview rubrics for Japanese PM candidates.

projects.name: Enterprise Ready Checklist
projects.description: Internal checklist template for B2B SaaS features targeting SOC2 and SSO buyers.

references.name: Kenji Yamamoto
references.reference: Tomoko brings clarity to ambiguous platform problems. She earns engineering respect because she understands technical constraints without pretending to write the code herself.
